summary: Israel intensified operations in southern Lebanon, killing battalion commander Dor Gedalia Ben Simhon and three soldiers, and striking over 80 Hezbollah targets. A ceasefire between Israel and Hezbollah was scheduled to begin at 16:00 local time, but violations continued with Hezbollah detonating an explosive near Ali Al‑Taher hill and Israeli airstrikes on Al‑Nabatieh. Regional actors, including the United States and Iran, voiced diplomatic concerns as Israeli actions affected broader negotiations.
key_points: Israel killed battalion commander Dor Gedalia Ben Simhon and three soldiers in south Lebanon during combat (Source: @New_security8200/201993).
Israeli forces attacked more than 80 Hezbollah targets in southern Lebanon, killing dozens of militants (Source: @New_security8200/201993).
A ceasefire between Israel and Hezbollah was set to start at 16:00 local time, according to multiple sources (Source: @kianmeli1/88086).
Hezbollah detonated an explosive device against Israeli forces near Ali Al‑Taher hill (Source: @presstv/194806).
Israeli fighter jets bombed Al‑Nabatieh minutes after the ceasefire announcement (Source: @presstv/194799).
President Trump urged a ceasefire with Hezbollah and discussed the situation with Israeli officials (Source: @Faytuks_Network/4142).
Iran held large public rallies in Tehran supporting the Islamic Republic and its armed forces (Source: @presstv/194809).

sections.executive_summary: Israel killed battalion commander Dor Gedalia Ben Simhon and three soldiers in south Lebanon, prompting a surge in strikes that hit over 80 Hezbollah targets. A ceasefire between Israel and Hezbollah was slated for 16:00 local time, yet both sides reported violations, including a Hezbollah explosive near Ali Al‑Taher hill and Israeli bombing of Al‑Nabatieh. Diplomatic pressure grew as the United States and Iran voiced concerns over the ongoing hostilities.

points: Thousands of Iranian patriots gathered in Tehran for nightly Muharram rallies supporting the Islamic Republic and its armed forces (Source: @presstv/194809).
Iran's Foreign Ministry said a planned meeting in Switzerland to discuss the Iran‑US MoU was no longer urgent due to Israel's continued strikes on Lebanon (Source: @presstv/194807).
Senior Iranian advisor Mohsen Rezaei claimed the US‑Israeli coalition lost control after fifteen days of the third imposed war on Iran (Source: @presstv/194811).
Iranian officials indicated that the digital signing of the Iran‑US MoU was completed, but technical negotiations would continue (Source: @Faytuks_Network/4138).
Israeli forces killed Sg Lt Dor Gedalia Ben Simhon, commander of Battalion 52, and three other soldiers in south Lebanon (Source: @New_security8200/201993).
The IDF reported striking more than 80 Hezbollah targets in southern Lebanon, eliminating dozens of militants (Source: @New_security8200/201993).
Israeli spokesperson Brig. Gen. Effie Defrin said troops have "full freedom of action" to destroy Hezbollah tunnel systems at Beaufort Castle and Ali Al‑Taher ridge (Source: @shin_persian).
Israeli fighter jets bombed Al‑Nabatieh shortly after the ceasefire announcement (Source: @presstv/194799).
Israel announced it would remain in the security zone from the sea to the Bofar heights to protect northern communities (Source: @New_security8200/201993).
Hezbollah detonated an explosive device against Israeli forces near Ali Al‑Taher hill, a strategic elevation in southern Lebanon (Source: @presstv/194806).
A ceasefire between Israel and Hezbollah was agreed to start at 16:00 local time, according to American and Qatari mediation (Source: @kianmeli1/88086).
Israeli airstrikes hit the Nabatieh region minutes after the ceasefire announcement (Source: @presstv/194799).
Hezbollah reportedly targeted a rescue force with a booby‑trapped convoy in southern Lebanon (Source: @rasedal3ado138e/371710).
Lebanese health ministry reported 47 dead and 97 injured from Israeli strikes up to early afternoon (Source: @lelotsenzura/93105).

key_developments: Israeli forces killed battalion commander Dor Gedalia Ben Simhon and three soldiers in south Lebanon, marking a significant loss for the IDF (Source: @New_security8200/201993).
Israel struck over 80 Hezbollah targets in southern Lebanon, eliminating dozens of militants and degrading command infrastructure (Source: @New_security8200/201993).
A ceasefire between Israel and Hezbollah was set for 16:00 local time, but both sides reported violations shortly after (Source: @kianmeli1/88086).
Hezbollah detonated an explosive device against Israeli forces near the strategic Ali Al‑Taher hill (Source: @presstv/194806).
Israeli fighter jets bombed Al‑Nabatieh minutes after the ceasefire announcement, indicating continued offensive operations (Source: @presstv/194799).
President Trump urged a ceasefire with Hezbollah and discussed the conflict with Israeli officials, reflecting US diplomatic involvement (Source: @Faytuks_Network/4142).
Iran held large public rallies in Tehran supporting the Islamic Republic and its armed forces, showing domestic mobilization amid regional tensions (Source: @presstv/194809).
sections.threat_assessment: Hezbollah continues to contest Israeli advances in southern Lebanon, employing explosives and IEDs near key terrain such as Ali Al‑Taher hill, while Israel maintains aggressive air and artillery strikes on over 80 targets. The scheduled ceasefire remains fragile, with both parties reporting violations, suggesting a high risk of renewed escalation. Regional actors, notably the United States and Iran, are influencing diplomatic dynamics, but their involvement has not yet curbed the on‑ground combat intensity.
sections.regional_response: The United States, through statements from President Trump, has urged a ceasefire and highlighted concerns over the impact of Israeli operations on broader US‑Iran negotiations. Iran, meanwhile, has mobilized public support through large rallies and signaled diplomatic frustration by postponing a Swiss meeting, citing Israeli strikes as a barrier. Lebanon's health ministry reported significant civilian casualties, underscoring domestic pressure on Lebanese authorities to manage the conflict spillover.
sections.intelligence_notes: Monitoring should focus on Hezbollah's use of improvised explosive devices around strategic elevations like Ali Al‑Taher hill and potential ambushes on Israeli rescue units. Israeli operational freedom, as declared by IDF spokesperson Brig. Gen. Defrin, suggests continued deep‑strike capability in the region, requiring vigilance on air defense and civilian infrastructure. Diplomatic channels involving the US, Qatar, and Iran remain active, but any shift in ceasefire compliance could rapidly alter the operational landscape.
